description_long: This is a traditional Japanese-style pagoda pavilion with a single-tiered, dark-tiled roof featuring elegantly upturned eaves and a prominent ornamental finial at the apex. The structure is supported by red wooden pillars and beams, with white plastered walls forming the central enclosed section. Surrounding the main building is a wooden deck with a red balustrade, accessible by a set of stairs. A distinctive arched red bridge, separate from the main structure, is positioned nearby, suggesting a garden or park setting. The overall design is symmetrical and open, inviting visitors to enter and enjoy the sheltered space. The materials and color palette evoke classic East Asian architectural aesthetics, making it a striking focal point in any urban park or cultural area.
description: A Japanese-style pagoda pavilion with a single-tiered, upturned roof, red pillars, white walls, and an ornamental finial, accompanied by a red arched bridge. The structure is open and inviting, ideal for parks or cultural settings.
description_view_0: View 0 shows the pagoda from the side, highlighting the upturned roof, red pillars, and the separate arched bridge to the right.
description_view_1: View 1 presents the front of the pagoda, with the entrance stairs, open deck, and the bridge aligned directly in front.
description_view_2: View 2 displays the opposite side from View 0, again showing the upturned roof and the bridge now on the left.
description_view_3: View 3 offers a rear view of the pagoda, focusing on the back deck and the symmetry of the roof and pillars.
